# Phase 10 Meta-Governance Boundary Plan

Phase: 10.0.35 - Constitutional Meta-Governance Boundary, Self-Limitation & Anti-Infinite-Expansion Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Define how constitutional governance limits itself so doctrines, treaties, federations, survivability structures, and meta-governance systems cannot expand infinitely, recursively centralize, or become self-justifying authority.

## Scope

- Constitutional self-limitation.
- Anti-infinite governance expansion.
- Meta-governance boundary standards.
- Governance scope containment.
- Anti-recursive authority.
- Institutional self-restraint.
- Bounded constitutional evolution.
- Anti-totalizing safeguards.
- Authority scope expiration.
- Governance humility boundaries.
- Finite governance continuity.
- Constitutional anti-expansion containment.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous meta-governance systems.
- Self-expanding governance systems.
- Recursive authority systems.
- Centralized constitutional authority.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- Every authority structure has scope, non-scope, review, challenge, and expiration or renewal logic.
- Recursive governance expansion is prohibited.
- Governance totalization is explicitly blocked.
- Self-limitation remains fail-closed under ambiguity.
- Runtime and deployment authority remain prohibited.
